title: Inga leptophylla
intro: Inga leptophylla, also known as "Palo Santo", is a plant species native to the tropical forests of Central and South America, belonging to the family Fabaceae. Traditionally, Inga leptophylla is used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal effects. Inga leptophylla is often used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are valued for their potential health benefits and are used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments.
taxonomy: Inga leptophylla is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae, commonly known as legumes. It belongs to the genus Inga, which comprises over 300 species of tropical trees. The genus Inga is a member of the tribe Ingeae, within the subfamily Faboideae. This classification is based on the plant's morphological characteristics and molecular phylogenetic studies. Inga leptophylla is native to Central and South America, where it grows as a tree or shrub in tropical forests.
